claims.evidence_text: The length of the total growing period of spring wheat ranges from 100 to 130 days while winter wheat needs about 180 to 250 days to mature.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat total growing period 100-130 days

claims.evidence_text: winter wheat needs about 180 to 250 days to mature.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat maturity duration 180-250 days

claims.evidence_text: Winter wheat requires a cold period or chilling (vernalization) during early growth for normal heading under long days.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat requires vernalization (chilling) for normal heading

claims.evidence_text: Winter wheat in its early stages of development exhibits a strong resistance to frost, down to - 20°C.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat early-stage frost resistance down to -20 C

claims.evidence_text: The resistance is lost in the active growth period in spring and during head development and flowering periods frost may lead to head sterility.
claims.claim_summary: Frost during head development and flowering causes head sterility

claims.evidence_text: For winter and spring wheat minimum daily temperature for measurable growth is about 5°C.
claims.claim_summary: Minimum daily temperature for measurable growth ~5 C

claims.evidence_text: Mean daily temperature for optimum growth and tillering is between 15 and 20°C.
claims.claim_summary: Optimum growth and tillering mean daily temperature 15-20 C

claims.evidence_text: The optimum pH ranges from 6 to 8.
claims.claim_summary: Optimum soil pH 6-8

claims.evidence_text: The crop can be grown on a wide range of soils but medium textures are preferred. Peaty soils containing high sodium, magnesium or iron should be avoided.
claims.claim_summary: Wide range of soils tolerated, medium textures preferred; avoid peaty high-Na/Mg/Fe soils

claims.evidence_text: For good yields the fertilizer requirements are up to 150 kg/ha N, 35 to 45 kg/ha P and 25 to 50 kg/ha K.
claims.claim_summary: Nitrogen requirement up to 150 kg/ha N

claims.evidence_text: For good yields the fertilizer requirements are up to 150 kg/ha N, 35 to 45 kg/ha P and 25 to 50 kg/ha K.
claims.claim_summary: Phosphorus requirement 35-45 kg/ha P

claims.evidence_text: For good yields the fertilizer requirements are up to 150 kg/ha N, 35 to 45 kg/ha P and 25 to 50 kg/ha K.
claims.claim_summary: Potassium requirement 25-50 kg/ha K

claims.evidence_text: for sandy loam to silt loam a depth of groundwater of 0.6 to 0.8 m can usually be tolerated, and for clay 0.8 to 1 m.
claims.claim_summary: Tolerable groundwater depth 0.6-0.8 m (sandy loam to silt loam)

claims.evidence_text: The crop is moderately tolerant to soil salinity but the ECe should not exceed 4 mmhos/cm in the upper soil layer during germination.
claims.claim_summary: ECe should not exceed 4 mmhos/cm during germination

claims.evidence_text: Yield decrease due to salinity is 0% at ECe 6.0, 10/ at 7.4, 25% at 9. 5, 50% at 13 and 100% at ECe 20 mmhos/cm.
claims.claim_summary: Salinity yield decrease: 0% at ECe 6.0, 25% at 9.5, 50% at 13, 100% at 20 mmhos/cm

claims.evidence_text: With pre-irrigation or sufficient rain to wet the upper soil layer, seeds are drilled 2 to 4 cm deep. as against 5 to 8 cm in dry soils
claims.claim_summary: Seeding depth 2-4 cm in moist soil (5-8 cm in dry soils)

claims.evidence_text: Under favourable water supply including irrigation and adequate fertilization row spacing is 0.12 to 0.15 m (450 to 700000 plants/ha) but increases to 0.25 m or more under poor rainfall conditions
claims.claim_summary: Row spacing 0.12-0.15 m under favourable water supply

claims.evidence_text: row spacing is 0.12 to 0.15 m (450 to 700000 plants/ha) but increases to 0.25 m or more under poor rainfall conditions (less than 200000 plants/ha).
claims.claim_summary: Target plant density 450,000-700,000 plants/ha under favourable water supply

claims.evidence_text: Sowing rates under irrigation are 100 to 120 kg/ha (drilled) to 110 to 140 kg/ha (broadcast).
claims.claim_summary: Sowing rate under irrigation 100-120 kg/ha (drilled)

claims.evidence_text: Wheat is often grown in rotation and legumes, sunflower and maize are considered as suitable rotation crops.
claims.claim_summary: Suitable rotation crops: legumes, sunflower, maize

claims.evidence_text: For high yields water requirements (ETm) are 450 to 650 mm depending on climate and length of growing period.
claims.claim_summary: Seasonal water requirement (ETm) 450-650 mm for high yields

claims.evidence_text: The crop coefficient (kc) relating maximum evapotranspiration (ETm) to reference evapotranspiration (ETo) is: during the initial stage 0.3-0.4 (15 to 20 days)
claims.claim_summary: Crop coefficient Kc initial stage 0.3-0.4

claims.evidence_text: the mid-season stage 1.05-1.2 (50 to 65 days)
claims.claim_summary: Crop coefficient Kc mid-season stage 1.05-1.2

claims.evidence_text: The flowering period (2) is most sensitive to water deficit. Pollen formation and fertilization can be seriously affected under heavy water stress
claims.claim_summary: Flowering period is most sensitive to water deficit

claims.evidence_text: In deep soils, the active rooting depth for spring wheat is 0.9 m with a maximum of 1.2 to 1.5 m and a spread of 0.15 to 0.25 m in all directions.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat maximum rooting depth 1.2-1.5 m (active 0.9 m)

claims.evidence_text: For winter wheat, active rooting depth is up to 1.2 m with maximum of 1.5 to over 2 m and a similar spread.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat maximum rooting depth 1.5 to over 2 m (active up to 1.2 m)

claims.evidence_text: water uptake of the crop is little affected at soil water depletion of less than 50 percent of the total available soil water (p = 0.5).
claims.claim_summary: Allowable soil-water depletion p=0.5 (uptake little affected below 50% depletion)

claims.evidence_text: Under irrigation a good commercial grain yield is 6 to 9 ton/ha (10 to 13 percent moisture).
claims.claim_summary: Commercial grain harvested at 10-13 percent moisture

claims.evidence_text: The water utilization efficiency for harvested yield (Ey) for grain varies then between 0.8 and 1.6 kg/m3.
claims.claim_summary: Water use efficiency for grain 0.8-1.6 kg/m3

claims.evidence_text: Crop Coefficient, Kc Spring Wheat Winter Wheat with - frozen soils -non-frozen soils ... 1.15 1.15 1.15
claims.claim_summary: Mid-season Kc 1.15 for spring and winter wheat (table)

claims.evidence_text: Depletion Coefficient, p Spring Wheat Winter Wheat 0.5 0.6
claims.claim_summary: Initial-stage depletion coefficient p=0.5 spring wheat, 0.6 winter wheat (table)
